Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 10:11 ET
Negative $9.6B gamma amplifies moves while index/single-name positioning diverges sharply
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Dealers short $9.6B gamma — mechanical amplification both directions
  • 2. Index puts heavy (P/C 1.59) vs single-name calls (0.94)
  • 3. Core PCE Thursday creates time pressure on positioning
NARRATIVE — divergent positioning amplified

Dealer positioning sits at negative $9.6B gamma with SPY trading $12.76 below the $669.29 flip level, creating a mechanical amplification environment where any directional catalyst triggers forced hedging flows. This negative gamma regime coincides with VIX at 25.4 (neutral) and sentiment at +9, but the critical divergence emerges in positioning: index P/C at 1.59 shows heavy institutional hedging via ETF puts while single-name P/C at 0.94 reflects bullish stock selection. The 0.38 implied correlation (stock-specific regime) confirms this dispersion positioning as managers hedge portfolio beta while maintaining conviction in individual names. The flow divergence intensifies when examining block activity, with massive SPY put blocks (55,503 contracts at 660 strike) occurring alongside aggressive single-name call blocks in MRVL (5,625 contracts) and widespread NVDA/TSLA call accumulation. TERN's extreme two-way flow (+815% calls, +467% puts) signals pure volatility demand ahead of catalyst events. This positioning structure suggests institutions are preparing for macro volatility while maintaining stock-specific conviction — a setup that becomes critical with Core PCE data Thursday providing the next major catalyst in a mechanically amplified environment.

Layer 1: Sentiment +9Layer 2: GEX -$9.6BLayer 3: P/C split 1.59/0.94Layer 4: IC 0.38Layer 9: PCE Thursday

ATM IV = at-the-money implied vol  |  IV PCTILE = where today's IV ranks vs ≈90 trading days (green=cheap, red=expensive)  |  Spread = ATM IV − RV 20d  |  Rich: IV/RV >1.2×  |  Skew 90/110: P(90%) − C(110%)  |  SKEW PCTILE: vs ≈90d history  |  PUT/CALL V%: daily volume rank vs ≈90d history  |  vs SECTOR: ticker ATM IV / sector ETF ATM IV (±10% threshold)  |  Divergence: cross-signal flags (VOL HIGH/SKEW FLAT, CHEAP VOL/RICH SKEW, etc.)
